# multiverse — `multi`

> A git-backed markdown **second brain** for agents and humans. `multi` is the
> storage-layer CLI: it hides git transport behind `read` / `write` / `search`
> so an agent never thinks about sync, and it enforces the conventions that keep
> a knowledge base navigable — above all, **every note has a one-line summary**.

`multi` is meant to be driven directly by shell-capable agents (e.g. Claude
Code) — no MCP server needed. If you are an LLM with shell access, the single
most useful thing you can do is run `multi guide` (a compact, always-current
mental model + cheatsheet) and `multi guide --claude-md` (a CLAUDE.md block to
paste into a project repo). This file is a static fallback for when you cannot
run the binary yet.

## Concepts

- **Brain** — a directory of markdown notes under git. Every note carries YAML
  front matter; its one-line `summary` is the gate: judge relevance by the
  summary before reading a body.
- **Markdown is the source of truth.** Plain `.md` in a git repo — no database,
  readable and editable anywhere (an editor, `grep`, Obsidian).
- **Git is the transport.** The agent calls `read`/`write`/`search`; `multi`
  does pull/commit/push underneath. You never run `git` inside a brain yourself.
- **Scope.** The directory you work in is bound (via `./.multi.yaml`, walked up
  the tree like `.git`) to SOURCES (brains you read) and TARGETS (brains you
  write). Reads span all sources; writes land in a target.
- **Resolution order** (per command): `--brain <name|path>` overrides everything
  → nearest `./.multi.yaml` → the brain the cwd sits inside → the active brain
  in the registry. The `--brain` flag goes BEFORE the subcommand, like git.

## Install

Prerequisites: `go` ≥ 1.26 (and `just` if you use the recipes below).

```bash
just install          # go install ./cmd/multi and ./cmd/ingester → $GOBIN (e.g. ~/go/bin)
# or
just build            # → ./bin/multi
```

Make sure `$GOBIN` (commonly `~/go/bin`) is on your `PATH` so `multi` resolves.

## Recommended layout

Keep every brain under a single `~/vaults/` directory in your home dir, one
subdirectory per brain (`~/vaults/<brain>`). It keeps paths predictable, makes
backup/sync trivial, and matches the examples in the docs.

## Getting started

```bash
multi onboard                                          # interactive: new or clone
multi init ~/vaults/mybrain --name mybrain --split topics,projects
multi clone git@host:me/brain.git ~/vaults/brain       # adopt an existing brain repo
multi brain list                                       # registered brains (* = active)
multi brain use mybrain                                # set the active brain
```

`init` scaffolds governance docs, `Templates/`, a `.gitignore` (keeps large
binaries out of git), and `.gitattributes` with `*.md merge=union`, then runs
`git init` with an initial commit.

## The agent loop

```bash
# read — summary first, body only on confirmed relevance
multi list                          # index: every note + its summary (all sources)
multi summary "<note>"              # one note's one-line gate
multi fm "<note>"                   # full front-matter block
multi search "<query>" [--body]     # match path/summary/tags (+ bodies)
multi find --type reference --tag <tag> --status active
multi read "<note>"                 # the deliberate full body read
multi backlinks "<note>" · multi links "<note>" · multi orphans

# write — structured + auto-committed (--summary is REQUIRED, one line about contents)
multi write --title "<title>" --dir <dir> \
  --summary "<one line>" --tags <tag> --source "<where>" --freshness "<currency>" \
  --body "<md>"                     # or --stdin
multi append "<note>" --content "<md>"   # or --stdin

# transport & integrity (multi runs git for you)
multi sync [-m "msg"]               # commit local → pull --rebase → push every brain in scope
multi status
multi lint [--summary|--tags|--fresh|--kebab] [--json]
multi fix [--dry-run] [--keep-display]   # rename every file+dir to kebab-case & rewrite all [[links]]
```

Most read commands accept `--json` for machine consumption. Errors are
self-correcting — they tell you how to fix them.

## Front-matter contract (enforced by `multi write` / checked by `multi lint`)

```yaml
type: reference        # moc | reference | decision | hub | meta  (default reference)
status: active         # active | draft | deprecated
tags: [<split>, ...]   # content notes carry one split tag + topic tags
created: <YYYY-MM-DD>   # auto-filled
summary: One-line description — the gate readers read first  (REQUIRED)
source: ...            # provenance
retrieved: <YYYY-MM-DD># auto-filled
freshness: ...         # one-line currency/trust note
```

Standing rules: every note has a `summary`; every *content* note carries one
split tag and records `source`/`retrieved`/`freshness`; hubs and the
`[[wikilink]]` graph are the index, not folders. One fact per file; never
co-edit a shared note. File and directory names are kebab-case (lowercase,
hyphen-separated, ASCII) — `multi write` slugs `--title`/`--dir`/`--path` for you
while keeping the in-note `# H1` human, and `multi fix` migrates a legacy brain.

## Multiple brains (per-directory scope)

```bash
cd ~/projects/my-project
multi use work                                          # read+write the work brain here and below
multi use work --read-only                              # read-only: no writes land here
multi scope set --source work,personal --target work    # read both, write only work
multi scope                                             # show the resolved scope
```

`./.multi.yaml`:

```yaml
sources: [work, personal]   # read commands span all of these
targets: [work]             # writes land here (first = default); omit → = sources
read_only: true             # no write targets at all (overrides targets)
```

With a scope active, reads span every source (results labeled by brain) and
writes go to the target. Qualify a note that exists in several brains as
`brain:note`, e.g. `multi read personal:"Home"`.

## Optional: session-end ingester

`ingester` is a companion binary that runs on Claude Code's Stop hook and weaves
each session's learnings into a brain — extending and cross-linking existing
notes rather than dumping standalone files. It steers a dedicated,
subscription-backed interactive Claude session over tmux. The default target
brain is `second-brain` (`const BrainName` in `internal/ingest/brain.go`); the
recursion guard is the `MULTI_INGEST=1` env var; the tmux session is named
`multi-ingest`. See the README's "Ingester" section for the install runbook.

## Pointers

- `README.md` — full documentation, TUI control panel, ingester runbook, layout.
- `multi guide` — the canonical, always-current agent cheatsheet (run it).
- `cmd/multi`, `cmd/ingester` — entrypoints. `internal/` — implementation.
